steps.title: Convert years to months
steps.content:
We're given the growth rate in months, but need the growth per year.
Convert: $k$ years = $12k$ months (since 1 year = 12 months).

steps.title: Set up the proportion
steps.content:
The tree grows 51 cm every $m$ months. For growth in $12k$ months, set up the proportion:
$$\frac{51}{m} = \frac{x}{12k}$$
Cross-multiplying:
$$x = \frac{51 \times 12k}{m}$$

steps.title: Simplify
steps.content:
Compute the numerator: $51 \times 12 = 612$.
Therefore, growth in $k$ years is $\frac{612k}{m}$ centimeters.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
